mission.title: bank-vaults: Auto renew TLS certificate
mission.description: Auto renew TLS certificate. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current bank-vaults deployment
steps.description:
Verify your bank-vaults version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n bank-vaults -l app.kubernetes.io/name=bank-vaults
helm list -n bank-vaults 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working bank-vaults installation.

steps.title: Review bank-vaults configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant bank-vaults configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n bank-vaults -l app.kubernetes.io/name=bank-vaults
kubectl get configmap -n bank-vaults -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=bank-vaults
```
The Vault TLS certificate is valid for 1 year currently, we have would like to support auto upgrading this certificate via the operator. An initial implementation has been added: https://github.com/banzaicloud/bank-vaults/tree/tls-auto-renewal

steps.title: Apply the fix for Auto renew TLS certificate
steps.description:
**1st step** - I had the same plan, an annotation will be added to the secret which describes the expiry time and we just read this annotation, let's say 1 week before the expiry time the operator updates the Secret.

**2nd step** - To be honest, I wanted to just restart the Vault Pods in the StatefulSet (yearly 1 restart) by adding an annotation to the podSpec part (the same as for the TLS secret), which makes the implementation more neutral and cleaner, because in this way we don't have to

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Upgrade bank-vaults to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade bank-vaults:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade bank-vaults bank-vaults/bank-vaults --namespace bank-vaults
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n bank-vaults
helm list -n bank-vaults
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n bank-vaults -l app.kubernetes.io/name=bank-vaults
kubectl get events -n bank-vaults --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Auto renew TLS certificate" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: **1st step** - I had the same plan, an annotation will be added to the secret which describes the expiry time and we just read this annotation, let's say 1 week before the expiry time the operator updates the Secret.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with bank-vaults installed or the issue environment reproducible.
